items.name: agent-browser
items.title: Agent Browser
items.description: Browser automation skill for using the baked agent-browser runtime from an OpenCode agent.

items.name: ar-chaser-agent
items.title: AR chaser agent
items.description: Accounts-receivable agent persona: reviews Stripe invoices, sends escalating reminders, holds risky balances for a human.

items.name: invoice-math
items.title: Invoice math
items.description: Aging buckets, reminder tiers, and dunning cadence for chasing overdue invoices.

items.name: ar-chaser
items.title: Overdue-invoice chaser
items.description: Chases overdue Stripe invoices with escalating email reminders, reconciles payments, and posts a daily summary — holding large or disputed balances for a human.

envVars.STRIPE_KEY: Stripe restricted key with read access to invoices

inputs.label: How often to check
inputs.help: Defaults to every weekday at 9:00 AM.

inputs.label: Slack channel for the daily summary
inputs.help: Where the daily list and approvals are posted.

triggers.name: Daily invoice check
triggers.prompt: Review overdue and soon-due Stripe invoices, send the appropriate reminder for each per the invoice-math skill, reconcile anything paid since the last run, and post the summary to {{alert_channel}}. Hold balances over the approval threshold, and anything disputed or heading to legal, for a human.

items.name: dep-upgrader-agent
items.title: Dependency upgrade agent
items.description: Reusable-session agent that keeps a repo's dependencies current: applies upgrades on an isolated branch, proves the full suite green in the sandbox, and opens a PR — never merging.

items.name: dependency-upgrade-skill
items.title: Dependency upgrade
items.description: The weekly dependency-upgrade runbook: upgrade tiers, the verification gate, breaking-change handling, the ledger, and guardrails.

items.name: dependency-upgrades
items.title: Dependency upgrades on autopilot
items.description: A weekly agent that keeps a repo's dependencies current: it applies patch, minor, and safe major upgrades on an isolated branch, proves the full suite green in the sandbox, and opens a reviewable PR only when everything passes — never merging on its own.

envVars.GH_TOKEN: GitHub token with PR + issue access to the target repo (used by the gh CLI)

inputs.label: Repository to keep updated
inputs.help: The GitHub repo the agent upgrades and opens PRs against.

inputs.label: When to run the weekly sweep
inputs.help: Defaults to every Tuesday at 05:00 UTC.

triggers.name: Weekly dependency upgrades
triggers.prompt: Load the `dependency-upgrade` skill and run one complete upgrade sweep on {{target_repo}}. This is ONE persistent session re-prompted weekly, so resume prior work first: read `.kortix/memory/dependency-upgrade-log.md`, open upgrade PRs, and in-flight branches. Discover outdated deps; apply patch/minor together and majors one at a time on an isolated branch; prove the FULL suite green in the sandbox (install, typecheck, lint, build, test, integration, audit); and open a PR only when everything passes. A failing upgrade is never pushed — log it and split or defer with a tracked issue. Never merge the PR yourself; a human owns the merge. Update the ledger at the end.

items.name: leaderboard-agent
items.title: Weekly leaderboard agent
items.description: Posts a weekly ENG/PRODUCT shipping brief to Slack: a ranked merged-PR leaderboard, shipping streaks, what shipped, and the async standup thread.

items.name: weekly-leaderboard-skill
items.title: Weekly leaderboard
items.description: The weekly shipping-brief runbook: window, PR-count sources, the leaderboard + streak format, the standup thread, the ledger, and Slack posting mechanics.

items.name: standup-summary
items.title: Weekly shipping leaderboard + standup
items.description: A weekly cron that posts an ENG/PRODUCT shipping brief to Slack — a ranked merged-PR leaderboard with week-on-week deltas and shipping streaks, what shipped, and an async standup thread — and keeps a running ledger. Reads GitHub, posts to Slack.

envVars.GH_TOKEN: GitHub token with read access to the repos you count PRs from (used by the gh CLI)

inputs.label: Repos to count merged PRs from
Slack channel to post the brief to
inputs.help: Where the weekly brief + standup thread land.

inputs.label: Who to @-mention on the standup thread
When to post the weekly brief
inputs.help: Defaults to every Monday at 09:00 UTC.

triggers.name: Weekly leaderboard
triggers.prompt: Load the `weekly-leaderboard` skill and post this week's ENG/PRODUCT shipping brief to {{slack_channel}}. This is ONE persistent session re-prompted weekly, so resume first: read `.kortix/memory/weekly-digests.md` (last week's leaderboard + streaks) and last week's standup thread. Count merged PRs per person across {{target_repos}} with gh (excluding bots), compute deltas and streaks against the ledger, gather Slack context, and post exactly one Block Kit brief (header, snapshot, what shipped, ranked PR leaderboard, streaks, Slack signal). Then reply in-thread with the async standup prompt (mention {{team_mentions}}), update the ledger, and land a scoped `memory: leaderboard` change request.

items.name: support-agent
items.title: Customer support agent
items.description: Triages Plain support threads, investigates with the codebase and Stripe, and resolves or drafts a reply — holding sensitive actions for approval.

items.name: customer-support
items.title: Customer support on autopilot
items.description: An agent that works the Plain support queue: it checks for new threads, investigates with the codebase and Stripe, and resolves or drafts a reply — holding refunds and account changes for human approval.

envVars.PLAIN_API_KEY: Plain API key (read + reply in support threads)
envVars.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: Stripe secret key with read access to customers and invoices

inputs.label: How often to check the support queue

triggers.name: Support queue triage
triggers.prompt: Check Plain for new or unanswered support threads. For each, investigate using the codebase and the customer's Stripe billing state, then resolve the thread directly or draft a reply with the diagnosis attached. Hold refunds, plan changes, and anything touching a customer's money or account state for human approval.
